title: Winey Creamy Peruvian Palm Sugar Nam Tan Pip Alao Brasil Form (Pollo a la Brasa)
description: A whole30-friendly, winey, creamy, cozy Peruvian dish featuring palm sugar nam tan pip, wrapped in leaves and steamed to perfection. This dish is inspired by the traditional Peruvian Pollo a la Brasa.
instructions: In a blender, combine the palm sugar nam tan pip, chicken broth, red wine, garlic, basil, oregano, kosher salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, whisk together the ghee and coconut oil. Add the blended mixture and whisk until smooth.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and coat with the marinade, making sure that each piece is coated evenly.
Wrap each chicken breast in 2 large leaves and tie with kitchen twine. Place the leaves and chicken in a steamer basket over boiling water.
Steam the chicken for 30 minutes, or until it is cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the steamer and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Warm the lime juice in a small saucepan over low heat.
Slice the chicken and serve with the warm lime juice, garnished with fresh cilantro.
Serve the chicken with additional marinade spooned over the top, if desired.
